description: A bright-field transmission electron micrograph of the as-cast Fe–10Al–15Mn–1.0C alloy (alloy C) displaying a duplex microstructure composed of region A (α-ferrite/D03 phase) and region B (γ-austenite phase). Region A, exhibiting lighter contrast, contains coarse κ'' carbides (marked as K'') distributed within the ordered D03 matrix and concentrated along the γ/α interphase boundaries. Region B shows characteristic lattice fringe contrast corresponding to the γ-austenite matrix with embedded fine precipitates. The image reveals that the κ'' carbides have a darker contrast and appear more frequently at phase boundaries than within the D03 matrix interior. According to the caption and text, these κ'' precipitates are (Fe,Mn)3AlCx carbides with an ordered L12-type structure, exhibiting a specific orientation relationship of [1-11]κ'' // [220]D03 and (011)κ'' // (001)D03 with the matrix. This figure substantiates the key finding that the macroscopic microstructure comprises a mixture of (γ + κ + κ') and (D03 + κ'') regions, with preferential nucleation of κ'' carbides at ferrite/austenite interfaces.
